target.name: Mission Zero Technologies
target.founder: Nicholas Chadwick, Shiladitya Ghosh, Gaël Gobaille-Shaw

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Mission Zero Technologies is a Series A climate and energy company that has raised £21.8M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of four same-stage, same-sector private companies: Watershed ($1.8B), Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M). This reflects a broader comparable universe of nine private companies and one public equity reference point in the climate and energy sector.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. The four selected comparables yield a median valuation of $175M, with a range spanning $110M at the low end to $1.8B at the high end. This wide spread reflects meaningful heterogeneity within early-stage climate tech; the peer set excludes outlier Watershed at the synthesis stage to establish a tighter working range. The median of $175M anchors the central case.

The triangulated valuation range is $149M to $201M, centered on $175M. This implies a post-money valuation approximately 8x the capital raised to date. Key uncertainties include technology maturity and capital efficiency relative to peers, as well as the sustainability of comparables' valuations in a volatile climate-tech funding environment. With only one valuation method applied, the analysis would benefit from revenue-multiple or DCF approaches if operational data becomes available.
